column1: # Expr-as-statement errnum is ARG-COUNT driven, not a per-builtin flag (bead sb-interpreter-imk).
# hw_verified via the sb-oracle skill (real SB 3.6.0), 2026-06-27. Captured with the
# self-validating method: consecutive cases have distinct expected errnums, so each F2 ERRNUM
# read differs from its predecessor and proves it is a fresh (non-stale) capture. The 2026-06-26
# 5iu harvest's "per-builtin flag" was an artifact of (a) comparing builtins at different arg
# counts and (b) harvesting GCOLOR()/GPAGE()/GCLS()/SPPAGE() with EMPTY parens (3 for everyone).
# RULE: NAME(args) as a whole-paren statement, NAME a registered builtin:
#   args.len() != 1                  -> Syntax error (3)            [0 args or 2+ comma args]
#   args.len() == 1, value function  -> Illegal function call (4)   [handler discards return]
#   args.len() == 1, command         -> runs (NOERR / its own err)
# --- same builtin, arg count flips the errnum (model.tsv, self-validated) ---
# --- commands: 1-arg runs, !=1 arg -> 3 (corrects the 5iu empty-paren mislabels) ---
# GCLS()/GCOLOR()/SPPAGE() with EMPTY parens -> 3 for every builtin (argc 0), even ABS():
# GPAGE(0) 1-arg is the value-getter form -> 4 (was mislabeled 3 from GPAGE() empty parens):
# --- 1-arg value functions reach the handler -> 4 (math c1, string c3, anchors fresh) ---
# --- 2+ arg value functions as a statement -> 3 (their natural arity, still rejected) ---
